sections.paragraph:
λιθοξόος, ου, ὁ (λίθος + ξέω ‘carve’; Timon Fgm. 25 D. [in Diog. L. 2, 19]) sculptor (so Plut., Mor. 74d; Ptolem., Apotel. 4, 4, 5; IG III, 1372) Dg 2:3.

Timon Timon , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Ptolem Ptolem , math., II a.d.—List 5

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 595
